report_name: CISA Adds Six Actively Exploited Vulnerabilities to KEV Catalog, Targeting Fortinet, Adobe, and Microsoft

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: Generic threat actors, including potential APTs and ransomware groups, actively exploiting vulnerabilities listed in CISA's KEV catalog, such as CVE-2026-21643 and CVE-2020-9715.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used to exploit the SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-21643) in the internet-facing Fortinet FortiClient EMS via a specially crafted HTTP request to achieve unauthorized code execution.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Client Execution
attack_patterns.description: Used to exploit the use-after-free vulnerability (CVE-2020-9715) in Adobe Acrobat Reader via a malicious PDF file to achieve arbitrary code execution on a victim's machine.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-21643
vulnerabilities.description: A critical SQL injection vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClient EMS that allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute unauthorized code or commands via a specially crafted HTTP request, potentially leading to full system compromise. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild.
vulnerabilities.kev_required_action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.
vulnerabilities.cisa_vuln_name: Fortinet FortiClient EMS SQL Injection Vulnerability

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2020-9715
vulnerabilities.description: A high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in Adobe Acrobat Reader that could allow an attacker to execute arbitrary code by tricking a victim into opening a malicious PDF file. This vulnerability is actively exploited in the wild.

identities.name: Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies
identities.description: A class of U.S. government organizations mandated by CISA to patch the newly added KEV vulnerabilities by April 27, 2026.

indicators.name: Potential Fortinet FortiClient EMS SQL Injection Attempt (CVE-2026-21643)
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Fortinet FortiClient EMS SQL Injection Attempt (CVE-2026-21643)
id: 5e9a3b1c-8d7e-4f6a-9b0c-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects HTTP requests to FortiClient EMS containing common SQL injection keywords, potentially indicating an exploitation attempt of CVE-2026-21643.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/cisa-adds-6-known-exploited-flaws-in.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
  - cve.2026.21643
level: high
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-query|contains:
      - 'UNION'
      - 'SELECT'
      - 'char('
      - "'"
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This indicator detects web requests with SQL syntax targeting FortiClient EMS, which could be an attempt to exploit CVE-2026-21643.

indicators.name: Anomalous Child Process from FortiClient EMS (Fms.exe)
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Child Process from FortiClient EMS (Fms.exe)
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the main FortiClient EMS process (Fms.exe) spawning shell processes, which may indicate successful exploitation of CVE-2026-21643.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/cisa-adds-6-known-exploited-flaws-in.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.execution
  - cve.2026.21643
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\Fms.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This indicator detects the FortiClient EMS process spawning command shells, a potential sign of post-exploitation activity related to CVE-2026-21643.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process from Adobe Reader (AcroRd32.exe)
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process from Adobe Reader (AcroRd32.exe)
id: f8e7d6c5-b4a3-4f2e-1d0c-9b8a7f6e5d4c
status: experimental
description: Detects Adobe Reader (AcroRd32.exe) spawning suspicious child processes, which may indicate successful exploitation of a vulnerability like CVE-2020-9715 via a malicious PDF.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/cisa-adds-6-known-exploited-flaws-in.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.execution
  - cve.2020.9715
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\AcroRd32.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This indicator detects Adobe Reader spawning command shells or script interpreters, a common post-exploitation behavior after exploiting vulnerabilities like CVE-2020-9715.

indicators.name: Monitored Processes for KEV Exploitation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Monitored Processes for KEV Exploitation
id: 3c2b1a98-7654-4fed-ba98-7654321fedcb
status: experimental
description: Monitors for the execution of processes related to recently listed KEV vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-21643, CVE-2020-9715) for further behavioral analysis.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/cisa-adds-6-known-exploited-flaws-in.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1203
level: informational
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Fms.exe'
      - '\AcroRd32.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This IOC-based indicator detects the execution of Fortinet EMS or Adobe Reader processes, which are targets of the specified KEV vulnerabilities.
